description:
## What

Replace coarse `cont_target_fns: HashSet<FnId>` (built at `src/ir_codegen.rs:1258-1272`) with a per-spec set `uniform_cont_reachable: HashSet<SpecId>` covering only specs that can have their slot 1 written by an unconditional-Tagged writer.

Then, at the two sites that consume the coarse set:
- `src/ir_codegen.rs:1440-1447` (schema build): force `kinds[0] = FieldKind::FzValue` iff `uniform_cont_reachable.contains(&spec_id)`.
- `src/ir_codegen.rs:1653-1656` (ABI build): force `reprs[0] = ArgRepr::Tagged` iff likewise.

## The analysis

A spec S is uniform-cont-reachable iff any of:

(a) Some caller spec C is uniform (i.e. `!natively_callable.contains(&C)`) AND has a `Term::Call` whose callee is also uniform (so `emit_return` will write into the cont frame's slot 1) AND that Call's cont resolves to S.

(b) Some caller spec C is uniform AND has a `Term::TailCall` to a native callee (line 2868-2911 fires) — this writes Tagged into MY cont, so all specs that can be C's cont must accept Tagged. "Can be C's cont" = ∃ caller of C uses C as the cont in a Term::Call.

(c) Some `MakeClosure` / closure stub site has a callee whose native-path stub (line 3366-3371) returns into a cont — that cont's spec is uniform-cont-reachable.

Implement as a fixed point on top of `natively_callable` (already computed at line 1459). Per-spec resolution uses `resolve_cont_sid` / `resolve_callee_sid` (already exist in codegen).

## Tests (TDD)

1. **Unit (in `ir_codegen.rs` test module)**: synthesize a small `Module` + `ModuleTypes` where one cont spec is reached only via native chain. Assert it's NOT in `uniform_cont_reachable`. Add a second case where the same FnId has another spec that IS reached uniformly; assert per-spec discrimination.
2. **Schema check**: build schemas and assert the per-spec FieldKind of slot 0 follows the analysis (FzValue iff in set; RawInt/RawF64 per typer otherwise).
3. **Integration**: a program where some cont spec is reached via emit_return — assert it still gets FzValue (no regression).

## Acceptance

- `cargo test` green, no warnings.
- No CLIF golden regression in existing fixtures (this ticket alone does not change emission shape for add1.fz — that arrives in .27.14.2; this is the prerequisite).
- For add1.fz: `k_2_s3`'s schema slot 0 becomes `FieldKind::RawI64` and its native ABI slot 0 becomes `ArgRepr::RawInt`. Body still produces correct output (the .27.14.2 cleanup is what makes the seam efficient; until then, callers re-coerce at the boundary).

## Risks / verification done

- Cont frames carry resolved SpecId in header (`.29.12.1`), so trampoline dispatch is unaffected.
- `store_args_into_callee_frame` (line 3146) already coerces per `FieldKind`, so the kind-aware writer path is correct under the relaxed schema.
- `unbox_int` / `unbox_float` are already used inside fn bodies (line 2500-2510) regardless of slot kind, so body codegen is unchanged.

## Out of scope

Native-chain branch cleanup (.27.14.2).
Kind-aware uniform writers (deferred follow-up).
title: Per-spec uniform-cont-reachable analysis; relax cont-target slot-0 force-Tagged
